reviews.review_description: Late review but still one of the best mixed reality games here.  Definitely a favorite.  The weapons are on point and the zombies are relentless. Action pact fun

This is a good game....nice graphics & animation, ability to manually setup your room (actually that's both a pro and a con) , and overall "creepy-ness"! Sometimes trouble reloading the gun.  I definitely do NOT like having to wait for the "Signal" to build up though in the beginning (as so many other reviewers have commented...you would think the developer would change this). ...you literally have to stand around doing nothing for about a minute or 2 before zombies start showing up. Maybe that was created to build up suspense or nervousness...but 15-20 seconds would be enough....otherwise it starts feeling like it's wasting my time....or that the game isn't working right. For this reason I prefer one of the other similar zombie shooting games in the Meta Store (hint: initials S.Z.) that quickly sets the room up for you and quickly gets the zombie invasion going right away.
Very skibidibgood

reviews.review_title: Fun game, great value for the price
reviews.review_description:
Once you understand starting the game with the signal terminal (otherwise you have to wait a few minutes to start) and keep it activated while fighting off the zombies it gets really frenetic.
Had great fun playing it over and over again.
Love it

First I didn't get what to do with that transmitter thing...but after a youtube tutorial yo got it.
Really great fun...but sometimes it bugs...then everything is black and white...monsters keeps on comming but I can't shoot them or get back to main menue. Some sayed that you don't get enough time to reload, when new guns are comming, but I think it is part of the fun that you have to keep the stresslevel..maybe a pause button could be a compromise.
But all in all great...stressfull...workout...love it.

This is an augmented reality game, and it’s wild how the cartoony graphics of the game mix with my home environment. It does take some getting used to, but I don’t get motion sickness with this game like I have been the others. Weapon aiming is pretty forgiving as well.
Fun game and easy to play you need alot of room to comfortable play
But it gets a little boring after a while. But it is 100% worth the mony
Easy to set up, Scary fun to play.
G
I love this game is a good game it's fun and exciting keep up the good work

reviews.review_title: Great starter game for new VR owners
reviews.review_description: A good cheap first game that will get you used to some of the features of VR, just wish you could skip the delay in standing around waiting for the monsters when you start the game.
So cool
definitely a workout and a little scary
Worth every penny

Wish someone could shed light on the random you died phenomenon others have also mentioned. Seems to happen the most just after you reload late in the game?!?

Is this game still being supported? Updates? Or do we have to move on?

It’s a fun game well worth the $10. But it has some frustrating problems. When you try to urgently draw your secondary weapon or grab some ammo to reload your primary weapon, there’s a decent chance you’ll grab something useless off the floor instead, wasting seconds while the monsters attack you. Occasionally one of your weapons will simply disappear, never to be found again. Sometimes the game ends abruptly with the declaration that you are dead even though your health bar is full and there are no monsters nearby and you’re left wondering what happened?
Finally there is the long wait to start the game. After you press the button and load your gun, you stand there for a good minute or two just waiting until the monsters finally show up. 
With all that said, there is still fun to be had. But it would be a wonderful game if they fixed these problems.
Love the freedom, just need multi-room Mapping and it would transcend!

Very real, fun!
played with the whole family.

It's definitely a very enjoyable experience.
But my complaint is the “lying timer.”
The initial remaining time displayed is completely bogus.
During gameplay, the timer frequently stops, forcing you to deal with zombies for that extra time.
I'm having fun playing, but I haven't cleared a single level yet.

reviews.review_title: Really fun once you get it set up!
reviews.review_description:
I love showing this game to people because it's such an awesome mixed reality experience. Room setup can be finicky, but once your room is set up, it works great! 

At one point, a Zombie Kool-Aid-Manned through a "wall" that I only set up to define a boundry. In reality, it was a hallway + stair railing, but when the zombie broke through, my brain believed it had always been a wall. The experience is very immersive.

I wish there was a bit more depth because the gameplay gets stale after a few hours. Different objectives, wave types, or gun options would help here. But all said, $10 for several hours of fun was a better bargain than the movies, so I'm satisfied.

reviews.review_title: Can't refund after buying the cabin
reviews.review_description:
Update: So yes I purchased this thinking I'd not be interested in the full game drop dead the cabin but gosh darn it wouldn't you guess it that after fighting the enemies enough I decided I wanted the full experience and purchased the cabin... Should have done that from the start...wish I could get the 9 bucks from this back since my game budget is limited but I'll deal with it since I'm now outside the two hour playtime window...ugh....

 So warring to you all: you just might find your self craving the rest of the sandwich so to speak... I really believed I was only interested in the MR standalone version and I'm glad they have it for people to choose but sometimes it's hard to be sure until you pull the trigger on one. I think maybe it's worse buying home invasion and realizing later you want the full the cabin experience than it would be to buy the cabin from the start and only play the MR cause you still got a full functioning game with all it's pieces and it's there if and you decide down the line to play it, but if you do what I did and buy the MR home invasion first the app becomes useless cause it's included already with the cain. Just something to consider.

Anyeyas my other feedback: 

1 issues:

I really admire what this app trys to do but their are some technical issues like grenades killing you from what should be a safe distance and their pins not pulling out or the extraction"roap" spawning outside of a reachable area (should spawn right above your current in-game position I think. 

They talk about profile experience when you select difficulties but I see nothing of that sort of any leveling system on the stand alone game.



2. Wishes

Not all of the following may even be possible on the devs end seeing as how space set up  built into the Q3 is negotiated with an app but still here they are:

* Lack of customization in the scenario like weapons or items on each wave or variations of enemy frequency and types is a bummer and holds such a great experience back. there should be in the options a custom scenario option that allows you to pick your weapons ammo limits and drop frequency or something and enemy types beyond just pre selected difficulties. Like starting round one with a shot gun or just melee weapons and starting round two with only adding a SMG or something.

* We really outta be able to manually add 3d representations of extra walls of various hight and widths for multi room setup or  fake ones like adding them to a larger real room to make more interesting layouts and maybe toggle if they are indestructible or what not. This would also eliminate an issue of zombies walking through real walls that separate or divide a room in some spaces.. 

* I wish I could steach and minimize windows and such exactly as I'd like them but they seem to only support big and square window sizes, even the smallest setting is not quite enough for a few in my home so I just go bigger than it actually is.


*I covered it already earlier but man id like to be able to place 3d graphical object including (3d walls) tables and trash cans and whatever or point to an area and scale draw them out as I see fit, select the type of item it is and have a 3d visual put on place of a real life object or one I just want to add but is not in my actual environment.


All in all tho the game is quite fun and I kept wishing my battery lasted longer during my first try lol. These are just my criticisms that hopefully in the future or future games like this will become reality I hope.

Before the new update the game would use my room scan, placing the door in the correct place and matching my 2 windows.

1 a large big bedroom window, the other a thing single pain window.

Now with the stupid build your own room first i place the door (where the game used to already place it anyway!)

And then i place the window, just time wasting now placing, then changing the width, then height, then removing it to correctly line it up (which it did perfectly before!)

Then i go to do my small single window and now the create your own window only goes to a square!!?? Wtf! It worked perfectly before why add this rediculous update!!

How about this...

Put it back to using the actual room setup and matching it (like before!)

THEN add the option to add ADDITIONAL doors and windows if required or just wanted to add to the room to change it up, not FORCE the player to have to create the room 1st that then doesnt let you match the window sizes!!

Ive deleted it as its useless now.

Well done 👍

developer_response.body:
Hey Beatz, sorry to hear the latest update isn't quite working for you. We're going to renable the old method for setting up your room via the meta scan in addition to the new all-in-one setup system. 

Our attention wasn't to add extra frustrating steps, the intention was to help as many people setup up their room as possible. However, we understand that these extra steps aren't needed for players like yourself and will rectify this in our next update.

Thanks for your feedback.

reviews.review_description:
I'm not sure what's going on. The game just won't work for me. It acts like it's going to load, I can see my hands and the belt, but that's all I get. No buttons I press do anything,I can't interact with anything that did load.

I've tried uninstalling and reinstalling the game. I've deleted and resetup my room in the Quest 3 settings. I'm not sure what else to do. I've probably spent 30+ minutes of "game time" just trying to get it going. It'll likely be getting returned.

developer_response.body:
Hey Super, sorry to hear you're experiencing these issues. If you could join our discord and share some videos of what your'e experiencing this would help us a bunch. Soulassembly.com/discord-dd

In the meantime, if you could spare 5 minutes to provide more information e.g. Headset you're using, firmware version, and confirmation you've setup spatial permissions etc this will give us something to start with in reproducing this issue from our side, however, it could also be related to the firmware you are on (Try checking for updates there too!)

reviews.review_title: So cool, a completely new experience
reviews.review_description: It lacks RPG elements, such as being able to unlock new weapons or upgrade existing ones, because that would significantly increase replayability.

reviews.review_title: So much fun, well worth the $10
reviews.review_description:
While I can understand folks wanting more depth to the game, you gotta see this for what it is... a groundbreaking and AWESOME experience that is unlike anything else we've seen so far.

I love me a good VR shooter, but I've never experienced one in Mixed Reality. There's something so trippy and nerve-wracking about seeing zombies bust into your actual room.

Room setup was intuitive and easy.
Nice to be able to customize things. The more doors and windows you add, the more difficult and stressful the game becomes hahahaha.

Sure, the gun mechanics are overly simplified if you're used to something like Pavlov, but I can get over that. If anything, the simplicity is a plus when it comes to introducing new players to the game.
I played for about an hour, then invited my wife to play it. She has much less experience than me with both firearms and video games, but she picked it up really fast... managed to survive for at least 10 minutes—LOL—and immediately wanted to go again.

FWIW, we're playing on Quest2, so the passthru view is only in black & white, but... still awesome.

Looking forward to seeing more mixed-reality games like this.
Kudos to the developers, I'm a fan!!!
Very good
Very cool how it combines real with pretend because it really makes it more genuine to experience. Play it all the time. Wish I knew how to unlock the hardcore level though.
Game need more ideas and levels

Playing on the 3s.

About 85% of the time the radio reset signal button will not work now times that by 3 for the reset. 
EXAMPLE: zombies will destroy the tower, you press the reset and it won't register it at all. If you get one now do it again. Then again. And again. 3 times slapping/pressing the button praying for it to register while zombies are attacking you now.

I want to love this game but I can't. These bugs truly make it so frustrating to play!  

Most of the time it won't grab what I'm going for. 
Example: if my axe is on my hip and ammo is on the ground, I'll go to grab my axe (hip) and it will pick up ammo off the ground.

developer_response.body: Hey, please download the latest update as these issue should now fixed with the 'Home Improvements' update.

reviews.review_description: Wooooo free gun

Okay, I don't know what the point is in putting things in your room that you can't even walk to. Went through the whole room setup thing, placed the necessary room objects in the room, but then it only gives you a little circle that you can stand in and if you walk out of that circle then the whole VR breaks and you have to create a new boundary? Couldn't even walk up to the table that I put down to get my weapons or to turn on the beacon because it requires me to step out of the circle so everything just disappears. You can't move around when you're playing the game or everything just disappears so you find yourself being killed and yet you can't even see what's killing you. I tried it more than once just to see if it would fix like it was just a one-time thing, but it wasn't. 

Just tried it again, after completely wiping all of the room data out of my device and resetting up the room. It seemed like it was working this time at least I was able to get to things but only because I pretty much put them right in front of me. But the pass-through is just glitchy as heck. Like there are tables and things like that in my room, which the room set up properly located and marked and yet creatures coming in would just walk right through them. I'm thinking this game would do much, much better if it just gave up on the idea of it being a mixed reality game, and had the same setup but immersive. Let me set up a boundary  in one of my rooms, place doors and windows where I want them, create fake walls and stuff in the game, let me place doors and windows where I want them to be, etc. And then instead of trying to mix the game stuff with my house contents, which clearly doesn't work, I can just be inside a game-built room doing the same thing and it would probably be much smoother. The height adjustment is useless. It plates the belt down by my knees, so I have to go into settings and adjust that, The game hands keep glitching with my actual hands and arms. It's very hard to stay " In the game" with all this glitching. I have creatures I can't attack because they're on the floor moving through my tables and stuff so if I try to I'm hitting my furniture.

Overall, I think the concept of this game is great, It just needs to definitely be redone, preferably not as a mixed reality if you can't figure out this mixed reality disaster.  Maybe it's just me, I don't know because I've seen some reviews on here saying it's great, but I'm not seeing even good. The graphics are great when they're working, But that's about it at the moment.

I've followed the directions. Scanned the room multiple times and added multiple doors and windows. Still nothing. I was so excited for this game, but it's caused nothing but frustration and disappointment. Seems I'm not the only one having this problem, but the recommended fix yields zero results.

developer_response.body: Hey, please download the latest update as these issue should now fixed with the 'Home Improvements' update.

reviews.review_description: Awesome game hours of fun

This is almost a 5 star. Fun game with people taking turns trying. There’s not a lot of depth to the game but it is fun. My biggest complaint: and others who tried the game is the hit detection. Hitting the button on the com is hit or miss. Shooting at the zombie at a distance sometimes doesn’t register even when you aim well. But it’s a lot of fun either way
